rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Validate that AGREE expressions have correct types; resolve '<error>' types by correcting root reference issues or removing invalid clauses.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match AADL expectations (int, real, bool) when declaring constants or equations.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
AGREE expressions must conform to strict type checking; mismatched types often indicate unresolved references or incorrect declarations.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless they are explicitly imported via 'with' clauses in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL component scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports should be placed in component type scopes, not implementations.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex to avoid 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or property associations that are not already present in the AADL model.
Avoid referencing identifiers or types that are not declared or imported in the current AADL context.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
